input.background: In the late 1970s, the United States male population between 20-29-years of age had body weights with an approximate lognormal distribution with mean $\mu=170$ pounds and standard deviation $\sigma=40$. To test whether body weight has increased since that time, we take an SRS of men from the current population, calculate their mean weight, and compare it to the historical mean.
input.question: We may also consider a twosided alternative that looks for both higher- and lower-than-expected population means.

output.answer: For this example, the two-sided alternative hypothesis would be $H_{\mathrm{a}}: \mu \neq 170$.

historical_mean_weight.description: The historical mean body weight of the US male population aged 20-29 in the late 1970s.

historical_standard_deviation.description: The historical standard deviation of body weight of the US male population aged 20-29 in the late 1970s.

current_mean_weight.description: The mean body weight of a simple random sample of the current US male population aged 20-29.

meta_info.caption: Null and alternative hypotheses (Body weight of  20-29-year-old U.S. men).
